airports.title: Newark · EWR
airports.body: The closest major hub — roughly a **1-hour** drive to Whitehouse Station, traffic permitting. Direct flights from most of the country and much of Europe.

airports.title: Philadelphia · PHL
airports.body: About **1 hr 15 min** away, and sometimes cheaper. JFK and LaGuardia are doable too (≈ 1 hr 30 min) if the fare is right.

airports.title: Rent a car
airports.body: You’ll want one out here — the venue and hotels are spread across the countryside and rideshares are scarce. Grab a car at the airport.

airports.title: Thursday or Friday AM
airports.body: It’s a Friday wedding, so plan to be settled by Friday midday. Thursday-night arrivals get the most relaxed start.

hotels.name: The Ryland Inn
hotels.notes: A restored country estate with the best dinner for miles, and the shortest trip home at the end of the night. Mention “Ghosh–Donnelly” when our block opens.

hotels.name: Hampton Inn, Clinton
hotels.notes: The dependable, value-friendly choice — free breakfast, easy parking, and a straight shot to the venue. Ideal for families.

hotels.name: Stays in Lambertville
hotels.notes: For anyone turning the wedding into a longer stay, the river towns of Lambertville and New Hope are full of inns, antique shops, and good coffee — a lovely base for a few days.

todo.title: Wine & cider trails
todo.body: Hunterdon is quietly full of small vineyards and orchards. Old York Cellars and Beneduce are easy, pretty stops.

todo.title: Lambertville & New Hope
todo.body: Twin river towns on the Delaware — antiques, galleries, a footbridge between two states, and dinner with a view.

todo.title: Round Valley
todo.body: A startlingly blue reservoir ringed by trails. Bring good shoes; the color of the water alone is worth the morning.

todo.title: Farm stands
todo.body: June is the height of the season. Pull over for berries and cut flowers — this is the corner of New Jersey that made us love it here.
